lastEventSummary: feature completed: Advanced Analytics Dashboard in 0 attempts

latestReinforcements.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
latestReinforcements.summary: The active policy is satisfied for this feature: the new analytics HTTP endpoints are synchronized across runtime route contracts and OpenAPI (`openapi.yaml`/`openapi.json`), generated TypeScript client artifacts are updated in the same change set, and contract tests validate route-to-spec coverage plus error response schema mappings for the new endpoints. The feature is runtime-flagged default OFF and implemented on pre-aggregated, versioned analytics models.

latestReinforcements.name: High-Risk Write Audit Discipline
latestReinforcements.summary: The Advanced Analytics Dashboard implementation satisfies the active policy for this feature: it is runtime-flagged default OFF, dashboard endpoints are unavailable when disabled, analytics reads use a pre-aggregated warehouse with versioned metric definitions, and high-risk operational writes remain covered by immutable audit evidence containing actorId/role/entityId/reason/timestamp with correlationId linkage.

latestReinforcements.name: No Legacy Path Rule
latestReinforcements.summary: The Advanced Analytics Dashboard implementation satisfies the active policy for this feature: it is runtime-gated by `PRELAUNCH_ADVANCED_ANALYTICS_DASHBOARD_ENABLED` with default OFF, dashboard routes are not mounted when disabled (and handler-level access returns 404), responses include vendor/plant/time breakdowns plus versioned metric definitions (`key/displayName/unit/formula/source/version`), and query execution reads pre-aggregated warehouse state rather than transactional tables while governance workflows remain the authoritative action path.

latestReinforcements.name: Optional Feature Flag Discipline
latestReinforcements.summary: The current Advanced Analytics Dashboard implementation satisfies the active optional-phase policy: it is gated by runtime flag `PRELAUNCH_ADVANCED_ANALYTICS_DASHBOARD_ENABLED` with default OFF, dashboard routes are not registered when disabled (and handler-level access also returns `404`), metric outputs are versioned and include formula/source definitions, and query reads come from a pre-aggregated `OperationsAnalyticsWarehouse` model rather than direct transactional-table analytics paths.

latestReinforcements.name: Data Retention Enforcement
latestReinforcements.summary: The current feature satisfies the active policy: advanced analytics is behind a runtime flag default OFF with endpoint gating, uses versioned metric definitions over pre-aggregated analytical state, and does not bypass existing enforced payroll/order/audit retention-purge workflows, encryption/access-control coverage, or audit-query traceability for deletion outcomes.

retrySummary: High-Risk Write Audit Discipline: Payroll settlement replay inflates analytics metrics; HR sync analytics uses request intent instead of persisted outcome
